_README: Beaten All-Ireland SEMI-FINALISTS for the irregular early era 1887-1928, researched from per-year Wikipedia articles (June 2026). For 1929-1989 the two beaten semi-finalists are NOT listed here: assemble.py derives them deterministically as (the four provincial champions) minus (the All-Ireland champion and runner-up), which is exact for the clean four-province knockout era. In the 1900-1908 'home final' years the loser of the home final is included as a semi-final-tier team (they were one win from the All-Ireland final). quarter_final_losers covers only years that genuinely had an extra All-Ireland-series round. Some early names are uncertain — see notes. champion/runner-up are NOT repeated here (they live in ai_finals.json).
notes: 1887 was an open-draw all-Ireland knockout of club champions with no provincial championships; Limerick beat Tipperary in the semi-final while Louth got a bye to the final. Earlier-round (de-facto quarter-final) losers: Wexford, Clare, Kilkenny.
1888: championship unfinished (GAA American tour) - no champion, year omitted entirely.
1889 and 1893-1899: only Leinster and Munster champions contested, meeting directly in the final, so there were NO All-Ireland semi-finals in those years.
1891/1892: only a single All-Ireland semi-final was played (one province got a bye), so only one beaten semi-finalist.

1900-1903 and 1908: a 'home' series (four provincial champions -> two home semi-finals -> a home final) decided who met London in the All-Ireland final; London was the runner-up those years. The two home-semi-final losers AND the home-final loser are listed as semi-final-tier teams.
1906/1907/1910: London lost an actual All-Ireland semi-final (Cork/Louth were the other finalists).
1912: Dublin won its semi-final but was replaced by Louth for the final, so Dublin is not a beaten semi-finalist; on-field SF losers were Kerry and Roscommon.
1922: Sligo won its semi-final but Galway took its place in the final after a replayed Connacht final; on-field SF losers were Monaghan and Tipperary.

1925: Kerry and Cavan were disqualified after objections; Galway awarded the title with Cavan as special runners-up. Eliminated semi-final-tier teams (excluding champion Galway and runner-up Cavan): Wexford and Kerry.
Some early beaten-finalist/semi-finalist names carry residual uncertainty and may warrant correction; they are a small share of total points and do not affect the modern standings.
